title: Alzheimer disease portfolio case-study witness (v1)
description: In-silico simulator-consistency witness for the Alzheimer disease portfolio case study. HONESTY SHOWCASE: Alzheimer's only FDA-approved DISEASE-MODIFYING drugs are anti-amyloid monoclonal antibodies (CBER biologics: lecanemab/Leqembi 2023, donanemab/Kisunla 2024, aducanumab/Aduhelm 2021 — voluntarily withdrawn 2024). The long-approved cholinergic palliatives (donepezil 1996, rivastigmine 2000, galantamine 2001) are CDER small molecules but their acetylcholinesterase-inhibition mechanism does NOT map cleanly onto any of the expansion-layer axes (METALLODRUG / OLIGONUCLEOTIDE / COVALENT / BIFUNCTIONAL / sub-axes). RESULT: the portfolio has ZERO in-scope drugs + 3 CBER-UNPLACED + 3 CDER-but-no-axis-map — the most honest extremity of the UNPLACED pattern. The schema deliberately PERMITS in_scope_drugs to be an empty array (no minItems: 1) and ENFORCES the honesty acknowledgement of the zero-in-scope outcome via `zero_in_scope_acknowledged: const true`. This is NEVER a therapeutic, clinical, efficacy, immunogenic, regulatory, or portfolio-recommendation claim (g8 / f2).

in_scope_drugs.description: FDA-approved Alzheimer drugs whose modality maps cleanly onto an in-repo expansion-layer axis. For Alzheimer this is the EMPTY ARRAY — the honest result. The disease's three FDA-approved disease-modifying drugs are all CBER monoclonal antibodies (UNPLACED by criterion #4 drug-only/CDER) and the legacy CDER cholinergic palliatives have no clean axis mapping (see `legacy_palliatives_no_axis`). An empty in_scope_drugs array is a VALID outcome of the case study and is explicitly acknowledged in `honesty.zero_in_scope_acknowledged`.

not_in_scope_drugs.description: FDA-approved Alzheimer disease-modifying drugs that are HONESTLY UNPLACED — CBER-regulated monoclonal antibodies falling outside the hexa-bio drug-only/CDER criterion #4 scope boundary; no code axis. Same UNPLACED pattern as AXIS/HIERARCHY.tape @N genetic_medicine_status / @N adc_status. Three entries: lecanemab (Leqembi), donanemab (Kisunla), aducanumab (Aduhelm — voluntarily withdrawn 2024).

fda_center.description: FDA center of regulatory jurisdiction; CBER for biologics including monoclonal antibodies.

axis.description: Null — UNPLACED by design (CBER biologic, no code axis).

cber_reason.description: Per-row documentation (g1) of WHY this drug is CBER-regulated (monoclonal antibody = biologic).

unplaced_precedent_in_repo.description: Pointer to the in-repo precedent for honest UNPLACED handling.

reported_not_run.description: Reported in the witness for honest portfolio coverage; NOT modeled (no axis sim run).

status_note.description: Current regulatory status note (e.g. approved · voluntarily withdrawn · accelerated → traditional).

legacy_palliatives_no_axis.description: FDA-approved CDER small-molecule cholinergic palliatives for Alzheimer — donepezil (1996), rivastigmine (2000), galantamine (2001). These drugs ARE small molecules under CDER discipline, but their acetylcholinesterase-inhibition mechanism does NOT map cleanly onto any of the expansion-layer axes (METALLODRUG / OLIGONUCLEOTIDE / COVALENT / BIFUNCTIONAL / sub-axes). They are SYMPTOMATIC palliatives, not disease-modifying. This is the case study's complexity: in-scope-by-CDER-discipline but with no clean axis mapping — flagged honestly rather than force-fit. A distinct honesty const (`legacy_palliative_no_axis: true`) separates this category from the CBER UNPLACED const.

fda_center.description: CDER — small molecule under CDER discipline.

axis.description: Null — CDER-class but no clean axis mapping (axes are designed for new modalities: PROTAC / CAM / OLIGO / METALLODRUG / COVALENT; AChE inhibition is a legacy cholinergic palliative mechanism with no matching axis).

legacy_palliative_no_axis.description: Honesty const — distinct from the CBER UNPLACED const. Marks this row as CDER-class but no clean axis mapping (not modeled here).

no_axis_map_reason.description: Per-row explanation of why this CDER small molecule does not map onto any of the expansion-layer axes.

reported_not_run.description: Reported in the witness for honest portfolio coverage; NOT modeled (no axis mapping).

symptomatic_not_disease_modifying.description: These are SYMPTOMATIC palliatives — they do not slow disease progression. Not a disease-modifying claim.

cross_axis_touch_point.description: Honest acknowledgement that this case study has NO cross-axis touch point — with zero in-scope drugs there is no in-axis sim to bridge to anything.

zero_in_scope_acknowledged.description: Schema-enforced acknowledgement that this case study has ZERO in-scope drugs — and that this is a valid, honest outcome rather than a gap to be papered over.

zero_in_scope_is_a_valid_outcome.description: Honest framing: zero-in-scope is the truthful result for Alzheimer given its modality landscape (CBER mAbs + legacy AChE palliatives).
